---
title: "Analyse-Notebook"
author: "Jinwook Bae, Miriam Haf, Xuchen Shen, Andreas Sünder"
lang: de
format: html
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source("source_all.R")
download_data()

COUNTRIES <- c("Germany", "Italy", "Sweden")
COUNTRIES_FOR_LITERACY <- c("Thailand", "India", "Afghanistan")
YEAR <- 2023
YEAR_FOR_LITERACY <- 2020

world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
```

# Analysis of generic inequality data

```{r}
gii <- load_owid_gii_data()

gii[order(-year), .N, by = year]

gii.latest <- gii[year == max(year)]
gii_world <- merge(world, gii.latest, by.x = "iso_a3", by.y = "code", all.x = TRUE)

ggplot(gii_world) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = value), color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1,
    name = "GII", na.value = "grey80",
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  coord_sf(ylim = c(-56, 90)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Gender Inequality Index, 2023")
```

# Analysis of education data

## Completion rates

```{r}
completion <- load_uis_completion_rates()

# This will be removed from the final code, just check data availability
# for individual years
completion[order(-year), .N, by = year]

# Global completion
completion.global <- completion[year == 2022 & code == "SDG: World", value, by = .(indicator, sex)]
ggplot(completion.global, aes(x = indicator, y = value, fill = sex)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge", stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Global completion rates by gender, 2022")

# by country
completion.countries <- completion[country %in% COUNTRIES]
ggplot(completion.countries[year == 2020], aes(x = country, y = value, fill = sex)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~indicator, ncol = 1) +
  labs(title = "Completion rates for selected countries, 2022")

# over time (averaged over all countries)
completion.global_time <- completion[code == "SDG: World"]
ggplot(completion.global_time, aes(x = year, y = value, color = sex, linetype = indicator)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Global completion rates over time")
```

## Gender parity index

```{r}
gpi <- load_owid_gpi_data()

gpi[order(-year), .N, by = .(educationLevel, year)]

# we outsource the repeating tasks for each education level
# to avoid duplicated code segments
plot_gpi_map <- function(data, level) {
  gpi_filtered <- data[year == 2020 & educationLevel == level]
  world_merged <- merge(world, gpi_filtered, by.x = "iso_a3", by.y = "code", all.x = TRUE)

  ggplot(world_merged) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = value), color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.1) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      midpoint = 1, low = "#b2182b", mid = "#ffffbf", high = "#2166ac",
      name = "GPI", na.value = "grey80",
      limits = c(0.5, 1.5), oob = scales::squish
    ) +
    coord_sf(ylim = c(-56, 90)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Gender Parity Index -", level, "Gross Enrollment Ratio"))
}

plot_gpi_map(gpi, "Primary")
plot_gpi_map(gpi, "Lower secondary")
plot_gpi_map(gpi, "Upper secondary")
plot_gpi_map(gpi, "Tertiary")
```


## Youth literacy

```{r}
literacy <- load_uis_literacy_rates()

literacy[order(-year), .N, by = .(year, ageGroup)]

literacy.countries <- literacy[country %in% COUNTRIES_FOR_LITERACY]

# As mentioned by our adviser, we will skip regions for now
# literacy.regions <- literacy[ageGroup == "15-24" & grepl("^UIS:", code)]
# literacy.regions[, region := sub("^UIS: ", "", code)]

literacy.gap.countries <- dcast(literacy.countries,
  country + year + ageGroup ~ sex,
  value.var = "value"
)[, gap := male - female]
literacy.gap.countries.latest <- literacy.gap.countries[year == YEAR_FOR_LITERACY]

# per country (2020)
ggplot(literacy.gap.countries.latest, aes(x = reorder(country, gap), y = gap)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ageGroup) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Gender gap (male - female, pp)") +
  labs(title = "Gender gap in literacy rates, by country and age group")

# over time
ggplot(literacy.gap.countries, aes(x = year, y = gap, color = country)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ageGroup) +
  labs(title = "Gender literacy gap over time, by country and age group")
```
